AFTER that wait_for returns/times out, the v1.12.3 synthesis net runs `_synthesize_from_tool_results(...)` (480) which is independently bounded by `_SYNTH_FALLBACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0` (510, 569). The synthesis is skipped on TIMEOUT/ERROR (478), so it only adds time on the success-but-empty path — but in that case a child the model was told has a 'Hard wall-clock budget' of e.g. 5s (schema text, tool_wrapper.py:232-239) can actually take up to 35s. title: Orphaned in-flight subagent rows wedge forever after restart and permanently consume per-tenant quota (no recovery sweep)
proof:
CONFIRMED in current source.

store.py:80-83 — stalled is in-flight, never terminal:
  _TERMINAL_STATES = frozenset({"succeeded", "failed", "timeout", "killed"})
  _IN_FLIGHT_STATES = frozenset({"queued", "running", "stalled"})
store.py:149-153 — is_terminal()/is_in_flight() derive from those sets, so queued/running/stalled are all "in flight, never terminal".

store.py:267-296 (_prune_terminal_locked) only ever evicts terminal rows:
  terminal_ids = [rid for rid, status in self._statuses.items() if status.is_terminal()]
docstring (271-272): "In-flight rows are never evicted". So an in-flight row is never pruned.

store.py:251 (_load_from_disk) rehydrates state verbatim: state=raw_status["state"] — a persisted queued/running row comes back as queued/running with NO normalization.

dispatcher.py:205 — self._tasks starts empty every boot; a fresh AsyncSubagentDispatcher is constructed each process start (entrypoint.py:1896). So no live asyncio.Task maps to any hydrated row.

dispatcher.py:121-124 (DispatchOutcome docstring) — recovery sweep is explicitly NOT implemented: "the matching row in the store is flagged ``stalled`` by the recovery sweep (deferred — for W1.3 we just leave the row as-is...)". grep across the subagent subsystem confirms NO code ever assigns state="stalled" — the only stalled occurrences are the SubagentState Literal (store.py:75) and _IN_FLIGHT_STATES (store.py:83).

store.py:420-437 (count_in_flight_for_tenant) counts any row where status.is_in_flight() and req.tenant_id matches — so each orphaned row permanently consumes one slot.

dispatcher.py:303-309 (dispatch_async): active = await self._store.count_in_flight_for_tenant(req.tenant_id); if active >= self._max_concurrent_per_tenant (default 15, dispatcher.py:67): raise TenantQuotaExceeded — every future background spawn from that tenant fails once 15 orphans accrue.

Only manual remediation exists: set_killed (store.py:458 / dispatcher.py:331) flips to terminal but must be invoked per request_id by an operator. No automatic clearing on boot. Tests confirm the gap: tests/system/subagent/ has zero references to stalled/restart/reconcil/recovery/orphan.
refined_fix:
Add a boot-time reconciliation that demotes every hydrated in-flight row to a terminal state, since at construction time the dispatcher's _tasks registry is empty so every such row is provably orphaned. Two coordinated edits:

1) Reclassify stalled as terminal so it stops counting and gets pruned. In store.py:80-83 move "stalled" from _IN_FLIGHT_STATES into _TERMINAL_STATES:
   _TERMINAL_STATES = frozenset({"succeeded", "failed", "timeout", "killed", "stalled"})
   _IN_FLIGHT_STATES = frozenset({"queued", "running"})

2) In SubagentTaskStore.__init__ (store.py:182-190), after _load_from_disk() and before _prune_terminal_locked(), add a synchronous reconciliation loop (no lock needed — store not yet published) that flips any hydrated queued/running row to stalled:
   now = _now_ms()
   for status in self._statuses.values():
       if status.state in ("queued", "running"):
           status.state = "stalled"
           status.finished_at = now
           status.finish_reason = "stalled_on_restart"
   self._flush_locked()  # persist the demotion
Then _prune_terminal_locked() (already called at :190) will treat them as terminal and bound them.

This makes count_in_flight_for_tenant (store.py:420) stop counting restarted-orphan rows (freeing the 15-slot quota), lets the retention cap evict them (store.py:267), and leaves an operator-visible "stalled_on_restart" reason on the admin UI. Update dispatcher.py:121-124 docstring to reflect the sweep now exists, and add a test in tests/system/subagent/test_store.py that persists a running row, re-opens the store, and asserts the row is stalled + not counted by count_in_flight_for_tenant.

original.title: Orphaned in-flight rows wedge forever after restart and permanently consume per-tenant quota (no recovery sweep)
original.evidence: `_IN_FLIGHT_STATES = frozenset({"queued", "running", "stalled"})` and the retention pruner only ever evicts terminal rows: `_prune_terminal_locked` builds `terminal_ids = [rid for rid, status in self._statuses.items() if status.is_terminal()]` and the docstring states "In-flight rows are never evicted". On a process restart the in-memory `_tasks` registry (dispatcher.py `_tasks: dict[str, DispatchOutcome]`) is wiped, but `_load_from_disk` rehydrates `queued`/`running` rows. There is NO code anywhere that ever sets state to `stalled` — `dispatcher.py:122` admits the recovery sweep is `deferred — for W1.3 we just leave the row as-is`. Such a row: (a) is counted forever by `count_in_flight_for_tenant` (which only checks `status.is_in_flight()`), permanently eating one of the 15 per-tenant slots; (b) is never pruned (only terminal rows are); (c) cannot be auto-resolved — only a manual operator `kill` on each id will flip it. Since `_tasks` is empty at boot, every hydrated in-flight row qualifies. Either evict `stalled` as terminal in `_prune_terminal_locked` or move `stalled` from `_IN_FLIGHT_STATES` to `_TERMINAL_STATES` so it stops counting against the quota and gets pruned.
original.claude_code_parity: Claude Code's Task tool never leaves a delegation permanently 'in progress' across a host restart; a dropped background task resolves to a terminal/failed state. Here it wedges and silently starves the concurrency budget.
